hero.title:
Cut 3× Faster
with Mixed Gas Technology
hero.subtitle: The EUCHIO Mixed Gas Device delivers micro-oxygen cutting for high-power laser machines (3KW–60KW). Burr-free edges, faster cutting speeds, and ultra-low power consumption.
hero.imgAlt: EUCHIO Mixed Gas Device - Nitrogen Oxygen Gas Mixing Equipment for Laser Cutting

principle.label: How It Works
principle.title: What is a Mixed Gas Device?
principle.description: Our nitrogen oxygen mixing device uses micro-oxygen laser cutting technology to produce a precisely calibrated N₂/O₂ mixed gas — eliminating burrs on carbon steel while delivering 3× faster cutting speed than traditional oxygen cutting. Designed for high power laser machines from 3kW to 60kW.
principle.flow1Desc: Raw materials stored in tanks, pressure 20–25 bar
principle.flow2Desc: Converts liquid gases to gaseous form
principle.flow3Desc: Stable pressure + proportional mixing → N₂ 95% / O₂ 5%
principle.flow4Desc: Output: 12–16 bar, flow rate up to 200m³/h
principle.specDesc: Input: N₂ + O₂ at 20–25 bar. Output: N₂ purity ~95%, pressure 12–16 bar, max flow 200m³/h. Compact gas tank (capacity >3m³).
principle.dimsDesc: 800mm × 350mm × 1100mm, weight 90kg. One device supports up to three laser machines simultaneously (One-to-Three configuration).
principle.cutEffectDesc: High cutting speed + zero burrs. After installation, adjust ratio between N₂ 95% and O₂ 5% for different board thicknesses. Built-in program analyzes usage ratio and records gas purity requirements.
principle.compatDesc: Works with all major brands: HAN'S, DNE, PENTA, LEAD, HSG, and more. Compatible with BODOR, JIATAI, HG LASER, XUNLEI, and other global brands.

advantages.label: Competitive Edge
advantages.title: Why Choose Mixed Gas Over O₂, N₂ or Air?
advantages.description: Burr-free edges, 3× faster cutting speed, and near-zero electricity cost. Our maintenance-free gas supply system is compatible with Han's laser, DNE, PENTA, LEAD, HSG, BODOR and other major brands.
advantages.speedDesc: Increase laser cutting speed up to 3× compared to oxygen cutting. Example: 8mm carbon steel at 16m/min with mixed gas vs only 2–3m/min with O₂.
advantages.burrsDesc: Unlike nitrogen cutting which produces burrs on plates ≥8mm (12kW) or ≥10mm (20kW), mixed gas cutting produces clean, burr-free surfaces on carbon steel — no secondary grinding needed.
advantages.costDesc: Only 2 kWh per 24 hours — near-zero electricity cost. Unlike air compressors that consume 15–30 kW continuously, the mixed gas device operates with minimal power draw while delivering 3× faster cutting performance.
advantages.maintDesc: Power consumption: only 2 kWh / 24 hours. Unlike air compressors requiring filter/oil changes every 3,000 hours, our industrial laser gas equipment is maintenance-free.
advantages.oneToTwoDesc: Stable one-to-three mixed gas equipment configuration. One industrial gas mixing station powers up to three laser machines of different power levels simultaneously.
advantages.lensDesc: Air compressors risk oil/water contamination burning laser head lenses ($5,000–$50,000 loss). Our pure liquid gas source keeps your optical system 100% safe.

comparison.title: Performance Comparison: Mixed Gas vs O₂ vs N₂ vs Air
surfaceRow: May have burrs on thick plates

airPain.summaryText: While air seems free, your real cost is in maintenance, rework, and lens replacement. Mixed gas costs less in the long run — and delivers superior quality.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What is a mixed gas device and how does it work?
faq.a1: A mixed gas device precisely blends liquid nitrogen (N₂) and liquid oxygen (O₂) into a calibrated N₂/O₂ gas mixture (typically 95%/5%). This micro-oxygen mixture is used as the auxiliary gas in high-power laser cutting, delivering 3× faster cutting speed on carbon steel compared to pure oxygen, while eliminating burrs entirely.
faq.q2: Is it compatible with my laser machine?
faq.a2: Yes. The EUCHIO Mixed Gas Device works with all major laser brands including HAN'S, DNE, PENTA, LEAD, HSG, BODOR, JIATAI, HG LASER, and XUNLEI. It supports machines from 3kW to 60kW. If your machine uses standard auxiliary gas connections, it is compatible.
faq.a3: Cutting range depends on laser power: 12kW handles up to 16mm carbon steel, 20kW up to 25mm, 30kW up to 30mm, and 60kW up to 40mm. It also cuts stainless steel and aluminum with excellent results.
faq.q4: How much can I save on operating costs?
faq.a4: The mixed gas device consumes only 2 kWh per 24 hours — near-zero electricity cost. The primary savings come from 3× faster cutting speed (more parts per shift) and eliminating manual deburring labor. Gas costs may be similar or slightly higher than pure N₂, but the throughput and quality gains far outweigh this for professional laser cutting shops.
faq.q5: Does the device require regular maintenance?
faq.a5: No. Unlike air compressors that need filter/oil changes every 500–3,000 hours, the EUCHIO Mixed Gas Device is maintenance-free. It uses pure liquid gas sources with no moving parts that wear out. Power consumption is just 2 kWh/24h.
faq.q6: Can one device serve multiple laser machines?
faq.a6: Yes. The EUCHIO Mixed Gas supports a stable One-to-Three configuration. One mixing station can simultaneously supply up to three laser machines of different power levels (e.g., one 12kW and one 20kW).
faq.q7: How is mixed gas different from air cutting?
faq.a7: Air cutting produces oxidized, rough edges and risks oil/water contamination that can burn expensive laser head lenses ($5,000–$50,000). Mixed gas delivers smooth, burr-free, silver-white cut surfaces with zero contamination risk — and cutting speeds 30% faster than air.
faq.a8: Installation is straightforward and typically completed within one day. The device connects to your existing liquid gas supply and laser machine. We provide detailed installation guides and remote technical support for all customers.
